section_title: A Message to the House of David

versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 11 Moreover, tell the house of the king of Judah to hear the word of the LORD. 12 O house of David, this is what the LORD says:
and rescue the victim of robbery
from the hand of his oppressor,
or My wrath will go forth like fire
and burn with no one to extinguish it
13 Behold, I am against you who dwell above the valley,
you who say, “Who can come against us?
14 I will punish you as your deeds deserve,
I will kindle a fire in your forest
that will consume everything around you.’ ”

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 21**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **A Message to the House of David** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 21** remains the first control on meaning.
